stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The variety of birds that visit the backyard feeder each morning ______ a source of delight for Ms. Park.
explanation: Strip out the prepositional phrase 'of birds that visit the backyard feeder each morning'; the subject is singular 'variety.' Use singular 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Neither the coach nor the players ______ aware of the last-minute schedule change.
explanation: With 'or'/'nor' compound subjects, match the verb to the CLOSER subject: 'players' (plural), so 'were.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Each of the applicants ______ required to submit two letters of recommendation.
explanation: 'Each' is singular, so 'is.' 'Of the applicants' is a prepositional phrase that doesn't change the subject.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The committee ______ reviewing the new proposal this afternoon.
explanation: Collective nouns like 'committee' are singular on the SAT when the group acts as a unit. Use 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

There ______ several reasons to reconsider the original plan.
explanation: In 'there is/are' inversions, the real subject follows the verb. 'Reasons' is plural, so 'are.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The list of items that students need to bring on the field trip ______ posted on the classroom bulletin board.
explanation: Subject: 'list' (singular). Strip out 'of items that students need to bring on the field trip.' Singular 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Every one of the exhibits at the new gallery ______ been designed to appeal to children.
explanation: 'Every one' is singular (like 'each'). The prepositional phrase 'of the exhibits' doesn't change that. Use 'has.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Neither of the candidates ______ able to attend tonight's debate.
explanation: 'Neither' (like 'either,' 'each') is singular. Use 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The athletes, along with their coach, ______ traveling to the championship on Saturday.
explanation: 'Along with' is a prepositional phrase, not a compound subject. The real subject is 'athletes' (plural), so 'are.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Mathematics ______ one of the oldest and most widely studied subjects in the world.
explanation: 'Mathematics' ends in -s but is singular. Use 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The number of students enrolled in after-school programs ______ risen sharply over the past five years.
explanation: 'The number' is singular. ('A number of' would be plural, but 'the number' is not.) Use 'has.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

A number of volunteers ______ signed up to help with the river cleanup.
explanation: 'A number of' is plural — it means 'several.' Use 'have.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The scientific community ______ divided on whether the new theory should be accepted.
explanation: Collective noun 'community' acting as a unit is singular. Use 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Either of those two dates ______ fine with the organizer.
explanation: 'Either' is singular even though 'of those two dates' is plural. Use 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The box of books and supplies that the principal ordered ______ arriving tomorrow.
explanation: Subject: 'box' (singular). Everything between is modifiers. Use 'is.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The data from the most recent experiments ______ consistent with the predictions of the theory.
explanation: On the SAT, 'data' is treated as plural. Use 'are.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Here ______ the final report and the supporting appendices that you requested.
explanation: Inverted order: the real subject is the compound 'the final report and the supporting appendices' — plural. Use 'are.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Each of the exhibits ______ a separate label and audio track.
explanation: 'Each' is singular. Use 'has.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The news from the front lines ______ reassuring to the families at home.
explanation: 'News' is singular even though it ends in -s. Use 'was.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

My favorite kind of cookies ______ baked by my grandmother every Christmas.
explanation: Subject: 'kind' (singular). 'Of cookies' is a prepositional phrase. Use 'is.'
